Represents the date of first sale to the customer. The column in the Netsuite Customer table is a calculated column, and changes to it don't cause changes to the date_last_modified, by Netsuite's design. Per Fivetran, they won't pick up the updates to the column, so users should calculate the value for such columns, off Transaction in this case. In the event a value for the customer isn't found, it defaults to Customer table value.

Represents the date of latest sale to the customer. The column in the Netsuite Customer table is a calculated column, and changes to it don't cause changes to the date_last_modified, by Netsuite's design. Per Fivetran, they won't pick up the updates to the column, so users should calculate the value for such columns, off Transaction in this case. In the event a value for the customer isn't found, it defaults to Customer table value.

ID of the Transaction
ID of the Transaction Line

Represents the ID of the state associated with the transaction. If the transaction state ID is 108 (defaults to United States without a state indicator) and the customer's country ID is 230 (United States), it uses the customer's state ID  instead. One exception is McKissock brand which can have multi-state license products which retains the 108 value. Otherwise, it uses the transaction state ID if available, falling back to the customer's state ID if not.

Represents the state code associated with the transaction. If the transaction state ID is 108 (defaults to United States without a state indicator) and the customer's country ID is 230 (United States), it uses the customer's state code. One exception is McKissock brand which can have multi-state license products which retains the 108 value. Otherwise, it uses the transaction state code if available, falling back to the customer's state code if not.

Represents the name of the state associated with the transaction. If the transaction state ID is 108 (defaults to United States without a state indicator) and the customer's country ID is 230 (United States), it uses the customer's state name. One exception is McKissock brand which can have multi-state license products which retains the 108 value. Otherwise, it uses the transaction state name if available, falling back to the customer's state name if not.

Primary education type in Netsuite. Collected from Netsuite item table Education type in Netsuite. Collected by logic: 1. If item table has education type filled (one of fields primary_education_type_id or education_type_id), then use it. 2. If no course state code, collect data from course offerings:
  2a. If state over all offerings is the same, then use it.
  2b. If state over all offerings is different, then use 'mutlistate' value.
3. If both values are null, prmote null as final value.

Course state code in Netsuite. Collected by logic: 1. If item table has course state code filled, then use it. 2. If no course state code, collect data from course offerings:
  2a. If state over all offerings is the same, then use it.
  2b. If state over all offerings is different, then use 'mutlistate' value.
3. If both values are null, prmote null as final value.

If the transaction state ID is 108 (defaults to United States without a state indicator) and the customer's country ID is 230 (United States), it uses the customer's state ID instead. Otherwise, it uses the transaction state ID if available, falling back to the customer's state ID if not.

Represents the state code associated with the transaction. If the transaction state ID is 108 (defaults to United States without a state indicator) and the customer's country ID is 230 (United States), it uses the customer's state code. Otherwise, it uses the transaction state code if available, falling back to the customer's state code if not.

Represents the name of the state associated with the transaction. If the transaction state ID is 108 (defaults to United States without a state indicator) and the customer's country ID is 230 (United States), it uses the customer's state name. Transactions can be one of the following types:

 * Invoice: B2B sales or income (positive value)
 * Invoice Refund: B2B anything "not sales", including cancellations, returns, etc (negative value)

* In the datamart, "Order" and "Invoice" are classified as "Invoice", while "Refund" goes to "Credit Memo"

Represents status of the transaction to track progress and resolve issues: Status can be one of the following values:
 * Order: B2C/B2B sales or income (positive value)

 * Invoice: B2B sales or income (positive value)
 * Invoice Refund: B2B anything "not sales", including cancellations, returns, etc (negative value)

* In the datamart, "Order" and "Invoice" are classified as "Invoice", while "Refund" goes to "Credit Memo"

Total number of emails sent. Baseline metric for all rate calculations.
**KPI Priority:** 1 - Critical baseline metric

Number of emails successfully delivered to recipient inboxes (sent - bounces).
Key denominator for open rate and click rate calculations.
**KPI Priority:** 2 - Critical for deliverability

Number of unique recipients who opened the email at least once.
Core engagement metric. Used to calculate open rate.
**KPI Priority:** 3 - Primary engagement metric

Number of unique recipients who clicked any link in the email at least once.
Primary conversion/engagement metric. Used to calculate click rate and CTR.
**KPI Priority:** 4 - Primary conversion metric

Number of recipients who unsubscribed as a result of this send.
Key negative outcome metric. Important for compliance and list health.
**KPI Priority:** 5 - Critical compliance signal

Number of emails that permanently failed delivery (invalid addresses, domain issues).
Impacts sender reputation and list quality.
**KPI Priority:** 6 - Important for list hygiene

Number of emails that temporarily failed delivery (full mailbox, server issues).
Indicator of deliverability challenges.
**KPI Priority:** 7 - Deliverability monitoring

Number of bounces that don't fit hard or soft categories.
**KPI Priority:** 8 - Secondary delivery tracking

Number of recipients with malformed or invalid email addresses.
Indicates data quality issues in contact list.
**KPI Priority:** 9 - Data quality metric

Number of recipients who were already unsubscribed before this send.
Important for suppression list compliance.
**KPI Priority:** 10 - Compliance tracking

Number of recipients marked as undeliverable before this send.
Prevents repeated sends to known bad addresses.
**KPI Priority:** 11 - List hygiene

Number of recipients excluded from the send (suppression lists, filters, etc.).
Explains gap between targeted and sent counts.
**KPI Priority:** 12 - Operational metric

Total number of recipients initially targeted before exclusions.
Operational metric, rarely used in external reporting.
**KPI Priority:** 13 - Internal operational use

Number of send attempts that resulted in errors.
Technical metric primarily used by operations teams.
**KPI Priority:** 14 - Technical monitoring

Number of records targeted but missing email addresses.
Niche data quality metric.
**KPI Priority:** 15 - Data quality edge case

Number of times the email was forwarded by recipients.
Low volume metric, not reliable for analytics due to tracking limitations.
**KPI Priority:** 16 - Low reliability metric

Number of duplicate email addresses in the send.
Operational hygiene metric, not typically reported.
**KPI Priority:** 17 - Operational hygiene
Customer ID of the authorized customer to use the API

The Status field on order line items comes with the following values:
On Hold: The quantity of items currently committed to open but unfulfilled sales orders Returned: Return is the act of sending back an order item after delivery to the recipient. Return may be due to cancellation, complaint or wrong delivery Return Submitted: Return is still on process Cancelled: User can cancel one or more order items before if they're not allocated or fulfilled No status: No issues with the order item"

The date/time when the order goods are made available.

Status of the order containing the following values:
Order Submitted: An order created after checkout completes In Process: Salesforce is reviewing and processing the order On Hold: The order indicates a tax exemption during checkout Shipped: The product is in transit Partial Shipped: Refers to the delivery of an order in multiple shipments Completed: When an order has been processed, authorized and then shipped Returned: Return is the act of sending back an order after delivery to the recipient. Return may be due to cancellation, complaint or wrong delivery Partial Returned: Instead of processing a full return, only need to return one or more items from this order Cancelled: User can cancel one or more order items before if they're not allocated or fulfilled

Represents the date of first sale to the customer. The column in the Netsuite Customer table is a calculated column, and changes to it don't cause changes to the date_last_modified, by Netsuite's design.
